paper_id: Age and origin of the Ngankipari Formation in the Noarlunga and Willunga embayments  southern South Australia
tectonic_setting: Cenozoic Saint Vincent Basin, passive margin, influenced by faulting and glacio-eustatic sea-level changes

paper_id: Backthrusts in the Bowen Basin Folded Zone  eastern Australia  progressive intra-basin shortening or passive-roof thrusting
metallogenic_belt: Bowen Basin, New England Orogen, eastern Australia
tectonic_setting: Foreland fold-thrust belt associated with the Hunter–Bowen Orogeny, backarc extensional basin inverted during contraction

paper_id: Constraints of the paleowind directions on the paleogeography of the North China  South China and Tarim cratons during the Ediacaran Period
tectonic_setting: Ediacaran paleogeography of North China, South China, and Tarim cratons; low-latitude positions near equator and western Australia

paper_id: Distribution of critical minerals in the Mount Evelyn Pb Zn Ag skarn deposit  Pine Creek Orogen  Northern Territory
metallogenic_belt: Pine Creek Orogen, Central Domain
tectonic_setting: Greenschist-facies metamorphosed Paleoproterozoic sedimentary basin with I-type granitic magmatism

source.evidence: Metals sourced from granitic magmatism (Cullen Supersuite) and/or sedimentary rocks; Pb-Zn-Ag skarn associated with granite intrusion

transport.evidence: Fluids transported via discordant en-échelon fractures and shear zone; skarn alteration indicates hydrothermal fluid flow

trap.evidence: Mineralization precipitated in fractures and shear zone within limestone; exoskarn assemblage indicates chemical trap

reservoir.evidence: Discordant veins and lodes; host rock is limestone of Koolpin Formation

seal.evidence: No specific seal mentioned; post-mineralization calcite veining may provide some sealing

timing.evidence: Mineralization related to ca 1835-1820 Ma Cullen Supersuite intrusion; no direct age for mineralization

preservation.evidence: Deposit preserved at depth; oxidation at surface; estimated remaining ore

paper_id: Paleomagnetism of the southern New England Orogen  can apparently conflicting data sets be reconciled
tectonic_setting: New England Orogen, part of the Tasmanides, a Paleozoic convergent margin orogen

paper_id: Pinpointing regional unconformities in the Surat Basin  Australia  by statistical identification of neutron wireline log anomalies
tectonic_setting: Intracratonic basin, fluvial-lacustrine depositional environment

paper_id: Regional tectonics  crustal deformation and contrasting batholith orientations in southeastern Australia  why is Late Devonian central Victoria differ
tectonic_setting: Late Devonian post-orogenic extension in the distal backarc region of the Lachlan Orogen, with east-west extension and crustal thinning above the Selwyn Block.

paper_id: Weathered crust hydrocarbon reservoirs  review and perspectives

metallogenic_belt: Pine Creek inlier, northern Australia
tectonic_setting: Contact metamorphic aureole of Proterozoic I-type granite, within a fold and thrust belt

source.evidence: Magmatic brine from crystallizing I-type granite, with high F and Cl; metamorphic fluid from devolatilizing metasediments

transport.evidence: Upward fluid flow focused into bedding-parallel fractures; near-lithostatic pressure gradient

trap.evidence: Fluid mixing with reduced carbonic fluid from carbonaceous slate; reduction triggers gold precipitation

reservoir.evidence: Quartz veins in bedding-parallel fractures within hornfels; crack-seal textures

seal.evidence: Carbonaceous slate acts as a redox barrier; no explicit seal mentioned

timing.evidence: Peak contact metamorphism at 550-620°C, 200 MPa; granite intrusion at 1835-1818 Ma

preservation.evidence: Post-mineralization deformation and thrusting; some preservation of veins

metallogenic_belt: Fore-Sudetic monocline, Lubin copper mining district, southwestern Poland
tectonic_setting: Fore-Sudetic monocline, Permian Zechstein basin, passive margin setting

source.evidence: Metals sourced from ascending oxidizing fluids; copper leaching by saline waters

transport.evidence: Fluid transport via fractures and permeable horizons; diffusion/advection of H2S

trap.evidence: Chemical trap due to redox gradients; organic-rich shales and sulfate reduction

reservoir.evidence: Host rocks include sandstone, dolomite, and mudstone with porosity and permeability

seal.evidence: Impermeable horizons (e.g., clay-rich Kupferschiefer) act as seals

timing.evidence: Timing debated: early diagenesis vs. late diagenesis; low-temperature anilite suggests early

preservation.evidence: Preserved in mines; some alteration by recent mine waters

metallogenic_belt: northern Coast Ranges, California
tectonic_setting: fore-arc basin, strike-slip and fold-thrust belt, associated with Clear Lake volcanic field

source.evidence: Lead isotopes indicate crustal source, possibly Great Valley sequence or mixture of radiogenic and nonradiogenic sources; sulfur isotopes suggest magmatic sulfur

transport.evidence: Meteoric water circulated through sedimentary rocks, evolved to high δ18O; fluids migrated along Stony Creek fault and dilation zones

trap.evidence: Boiling and phase separation in near-surface environment; steep thermal and pressure gradients; hydrothermal eruption breccias

reservoir.evidence: Sheeted vein complex in dilation zone at rheologic contrast between competent basalt and incompetent melange

seal.evidence: Self-sealing by silica precipitation; sinter cap preserved

timing.evidence: K-Ar ages of alunite 0.75-2.2 Ma; maximum age from basaltic andesite 2.2 ± 0.2 Ma

preservation.evidence: Subaerial sinter preserved, indicating minimal post-mineralization erosion

metallogenic_belt: Cordilleran magmatic arc, Great Basin
tectonic_setting: Extensional to transtensional intra-arc graben within the early Mesozoic Cordilleran magmatic arc

source.evidence: Stable isotope data indicate dominantly felsic igneous source for sulfur; high K calc-alkaline dacite porphyry swarm genetically linked

transport.evidence: Fluids transported along permeable structures: Pickhandle thrust and lower Candelaria shear, enhanced by brecciation and delamination of host shales

trap.evidence: Ore precipitated in tabular zones along thrust and shear due to chemical (carbonaceous marly shales) and structural (permeable breccia zones) traps

reservoir.evidence: Host rocks include reactive carbonaceous marly shales (member 1 of Candelaria Formation) and mafic-ultramafic units; ore zones up to 30 m thick

seal.evidence: Overlying Pickhandle allochthon and less permeable units may have acted as seals; post-mineralization thrusts and later Basin-Range faulting affected preservation

timing.evidence: U-Pb zircon age of porphyry dikes at 192 ± 3 Ma; mineralization is syn- to late-magmatic, estimated between 187 and 194 Ma

preservation.evidence: Supergene oxidation to 200 m depth; K-Ar sericite ages (91-129 Ma) indicate partial resetting by Cretaceous thermal event, but deposit largely preserved

metallogenic_belt: Iron Springs mining district, southwestern Utah, Basin and Range Province
tectonic_setting: Miocene laccoliths emplaced in a continental arc setting, related to Sevier thrust faults

source.evidence: Iron derived from subsolidus alteration of quartz monzonite, with mass balance indicating sufficient Fe from selvage alteration of 40 km3 of intrusive rock.

transport.evidence: Fluid transported via selvage joints and fractures; Cl-bearing aqueous fluid with FeCl complexes; evidence from vein mineral assemblages and fluid inclusions.

trap.evidence: Strata-bound orebodies in Homestake Limestone; replacement at constant volume; precipitation due to cooling and possibly fluid-rock reaction.

reservoir.evidence: Limestone host provides reactive chemical trap; porosity about 20% in ore; volume-for-volume replacement.

seal.evidence: Peripheral shell and overlying impermeable sedimentary rocks (Temple Cap Sandstone, Carmel Formation) acted as seals; selvage joints did not penetrate peripheral shell.

timing.evidence: K-Ar ages of biotite from laccoliths: 21.5-22.6 Ma; ore formation postdates solidus, with calcite-dolomite temperature of 485°C.

preservation.evidence: Orebodies preserved at surface; some erosion has occurred; Stoddard Mountain pluton lacks mineralization due to different wall rock and shallower emplacement.

structural_controls: flow-banded and flow-brecciated portions of flows
metallogenic_belt: Bushveld Complex, Kaapvaal Craton
tectonic_setting: Intracontinental large igneous province, associated with the 2.06 Ga Bushveld magmatic event

source.evidence: Metals (Pb, Zn, Mn) derived from volcanic rocks and possibly from fluids originating from floor rocks and xenoliths within the Rustenburg Layered Suite.

transport.evidence: Hydrothermal fluids migrated through permeable pyroclastic horizons and flow margins, driven by heat from the Rustenburg Layered Suite.

trap.evidence: Precipitation of sulfides (pyrite, arsenopyrite) and enrichment of Pb, Zn, Mn in hydrated, altered roof rocks, particularly in porous zones.

reservoir.evidence: Roof rocks of the Rooiberg Group, especially felsites and pyroclastic units, provided permeable pathways and reactive host rocks.

seal.evidence: Overlying less altered volcanic rocks may have acted as a seal, but no specific seal is described.

timing.evidence: Two stages: early pyrite-arsenopyrite mineralization possibly related to granophyre intrusion; later Pb-Zn-Mn enrichment linked to emplacement of the Rustenburg Layered Suite at ~2.06 Ga.

preservation.evidence: The roof rocks are preserved in the Bushveld Complex roof, with only low-grade metamorphism, indicating good preservation despite later deformation.

metallogenic_belt: Cascade Mountains, southern Coast Plutonic Complex
tectonic_setting: Tectonic contact between Permian-Jurassic ocean floor/island-arc rocks (Hozameen Group) and Jurassic turbiditic sediments (Ladner Group), near Coquihalla serpentine belt

source.evidence: Highly evolved meteoric waters as ore-forming fluids; metals likely derived from host rocks or deeper sources

transport.evidence: Fluid flow along permeable coarse clastic zones and structural conduits (F2 fold hinge, faults)

trap.evidence: Saddle reef morphology in tectonically thickened hinge zone of F2 antiform; chemical precipitation due to temperature decrease (350-380°C) and fluid-rock interaction

reservoir.evidence: Permeable, competent coarse clastic zones (wacke, conglomerate) within Ladner Group; up to 30 m thick orebodies

seal.evidence: Barren alteration envelope with K and Ba depletion; overlying siltstone and argillite may provide some seal

timing.evidence: Mineralization between mid-Cretaceous and late Eocene; limited isotopic and fluid inclusion data

preservation.evidence: Deposit preserved at shallow depth (1-3 km); truncated by Richardson fault; some erosion but mineralization subcrops

metallogenic_belt: Manitouwadge greenstone belt, Wawa subprovince, Superior Province
tectonic_setting: Archean greenstone belt, volcanic arc or rifted arc setting, affected by polyphase deformation and upper amphibolite facies metamorphism

source.evidence: Metals sourced from felsic volcanic rocks and synvolcanic trondhjemite; hydrothermal fluids leached metals from volcanic pile.

transport.evidence: Fluids transported via permeable volcaniclastic aquifers and focused along D1 faults and fractures; stockwork zones indicate subsurface conduits.

trap.evidence: Chemical precipitation on seafloor or near-seafloor replacement; iron-formation horizons and alteration zones act as chemical traps; Cu-Zn zoning indicates temperature gradients.

reservoir.evidence: Massive and semimassive sulfide lenses hosted by iron-formation and altered volcanic rocks; stockwork zones in footwall alteration.

seal.evidence: Sillimanite-muscovite-quartz schist and iron-formation may have acted as seals; later deformation and metamorphism modified original permeability.

timing.evidence: U-Pb zircon age of 2720 ± 3 Ma for synvolcanic trondhjemite and felsic volcanism; mineralization is syngenetic with volcanic activity.

preservation.evidence: Deposits preserved despite polyphase deformation and upper amphibolite facies metamorphism; some remobilization but primary features retained.

metallogenic_belt: Al Wajh gold district, Midyan terrane, Arabian-Nubian Shield
tectonic_setting: Neoproterozoic accretionary orogen, Arabian-Nubian Shield

source.evidence: Oxygen isotopes suggest low-grade metamorphic fluid source; gold sourced from mafic volcanic rocks with elevated background Au

transport.evidence: Fluid transport along D2 strike-slip shear zones and D3 thrust faults; multiple deformation episodes facilitated fluid flow

trap.evidence: Gold precipitated in quartz veins within dilatant zones of shear zones and thrust faults; structural traps include fold crests and fault bends

reservoir.evidence: Quartz veins and sheeted zones in altered wall rock; limited extent and small tonnage

seal.evidence: No specific seal identified; veins are open to surface; sericite alteration may have provided some sealing

timing.evidence: Main mineralization during D2 and D3 deformation episodes, constrained between 700-660 Ma; minor D4 veins at ~620 Ma

preservation.evidence: Deposits are small and shallow; ancient mining to only 8 m depth; veins may pinch out at depth; no evidence of deep preservation

metallogenic_belt: Coeur d'Alene mining district, Silver Belt, Belt Supergroup, North American craton
tectonic_setting: Cretaceous accretion and suturing of exotic terranes onto western Idaho, associated with regional compression and formation of granitic batholiths

source.evidence: Metamorphic/formation fluids derived from Belt Supergroup metasedimentary rocks; high 87Sr/86Sr ratios indicate crustal source

transport.evidence: Fluids ascended along steep faults and fractures parallel to reverse faults; high temperature (325°C) and pressure (1-3 kbar) regime

trap.evidence: Siderite precipitation due to temperature increase and CO2 effervescence; U-shaped isotopic trends indicate deposition from vein margins inward

reservoir.evidence: Tabular veins in fractured metasedimentary rocks; sharp contacts with wall rock; vein widths up to 2 m

seal.evidence: No specific seal mentioned; veins are open to later faulting and brecciation

timing.evidence: Sr isotope data indicate Mesozoic or younger age; likely Late Cretaceous to early Tertiary; unsupported radiogenic Sr requires young formation

preservation.evidence: Veins are undeformed except for minor postdepositional faulting; preserved at depths of 3-10 km

metallogenic_belt: Cape Smith fold belt, Ungava trough, Quebec
tectonic_setting: Within-plate environment subject to hot spot volcanism in a distended continental regime, later ocean crust formation

source.evidence: Parental magma derived from ~20% melting of garnet lherzolite; high CaO content (7.21-11.26%)

transport.evidence: PGE transported by silicate magma; later migration of S-bearing liquid and fluid phases

trap.evidence: Sulfide precipitation due to decreasing sulfide capacity from CaO removal and increasing SiO2; R factor 100-1000

reservoir.evidence: Pegmatitic gabbro horizon as host; reef up to 1 m thick, 2 km long

seal.evidence: Overlying anorthosite and gabbro layers; no specific seal discussed

timing.evidence: No absolute ages reported; relative timing from crystallization sequence

preservation.evidence: Intrusion preserved; some hydrothermal alteration and carbonatized zones noted

metallogenic_belt: Kibaran belt, Central African tin belt
tectonic_setting: Mesoproterozoic Kibaran mobile belt, postorogenic extension related to G4 granite magmatism

source.evidence: Highly fractionated, peraluminous G4 granites (tin granites) derived from crustal melting, enriched in Sn, W, Nb, Ta, Li, Cs, Rb, B, F.

transport.evidence: Pegmatite lenses and hydrothermal vein systems along fractures and shear zones; fluid circulation related to granite emplacement.

trap.evidence: Pegmatite lenses with internal zoning; quartz cores surrounded by mineralized zones; hydrothermal veins in metasedimentary rocks.

reservoir.evidence: Pegmatite lenses up to 400 m wide and several km long; quartz-muscovite veins and quartz veins.

seal.evidence: Country rocks (metasediments) provide some seal; later brittle deformation allowed fluid escape.

timing.evidence: U-Pb columbite ages: 962 ± 2 Ma (Kivuvu) and 968+33 Ma (Ruhembe); Pan-African overprint at ca 620 Ma.

preservation.evidence: Deep weathering and Pan-African brittle reworking redistributed Au and Bi; primary pegmatite minerals partially preserved.

metallogenic_belt: New England orogen, Lachlan fold belt, eastern Australia
tectonic_setting: high-level epizonal plutons in a fold belt

source.evidence: Magmatic aqueous phase scavenging metals from larger volumes of granite; elevated Mo, Cu, Au, S, Zn in interconnected miarolitic texture-bearing phases indicate hydrothermal enrichment from magmatic volatiles.

transport.evidence: Interconnected miarolitic texture provides direct evidence of magmatic volatile-phase permeability and connectivity, enabling advection of fluids through spanning clusters.

trap.evidence: Mineralization occurs in leucocratic segregations and apical portions of plutons; precipitation likely due to cooling and decompression, but specific trap mechanism not detailed.

reservoir.evidence: Host rocks are granitic phases with miarolitic cavities and aplitic groundmass; porosity from interconnected miaroles provides space for mineralization.

seal.evidence: No explicit seal described; overlying volcanics and sediments may act as cap, but not discussed.

timing.evidence: Permo-Triassic age inferred from regional geology, but no direct age data for mineralization or interconnected miarolitic texture.

preservation.evidence: Texture preserved in high-level plutons; some erosion has exposed alluvial Sn deposits, indicating partial preservation.

metallogenic_belt: Andean cordillera, southern Peru
tectonic_setting: extensional normal faulting within a Neogene calc-alkaline volcanic belt, related to Quechua 1 tectonism

source.evidence: Lead isotope data and tellurium abundance suggest igneous source for metals; magmatic fluid component inferred from oxygen isotopes

transport.evidence: Hydrothermal fluids focused along normal faults; fluid flow steep, with some horizontal component

trap.evidence: Ore deposited at fault junctions, highly fractured wall rocks, and changes in vein orientation; open-space filling

reservoir.evidence: Veins hosted by early Miocene volcanic rocks; fractures and faults provide permeability

seal.evidence: Barren quartz caps at surface; alluvium covers some veins; postmineral tilting but no major erosion

timing.evidence: K-Ar and 40Ar/39Ar dating of adularia and alunite constrain mineralization at 17.8 ± 0.1 Ma, about 1 m.y. after Sarpane volcanism

preservation.evidence: Veins preserved despite postmineral tilting and minor fault offset; some veins exposed by erosion, others buried by alluvium

metallogenic_belt: Appalachian thrust belt, lower St. Lawrence area
tectonic_setting: Appalachian thrust belt

source.evidence: Pb isotope data indicate mudstones as major lead source; sandstones contribute minor lead.

transport.evidence: Hydrothermal brines transported lead; permeability of sandstone controlled leaching efficiency.

trap.evidence: Fractures and dissolution pores in sandstone served as traps for vein and disseminated mineralization.

reservoir.evidence: Sandstone porosity and permeability provided reservoir space; vein-only deposits have restricted permeability.

seal.evidence: Mudstones may act as seals, but no direct evidence discussed.

timing.evidence: Mineralization age ~450 Ma based on structural studies and model galena Pb ages; Rb-Sr dating of barite gives 490 ± 40 Ma.

preservation.evidence: Deposits preserved in thrust belt, but no specific discussion of post-mineralization preservation.

metallogenic_belt: Eastern Goldfields province, Yilgarn craton
tectonic_setting: Archean greenstone belt

source.evidence: Nd isotopes indicate komatiite as source of Nd and likely Au; komatiites have high Au content

transport.evidence: Fluid flow along dextral faults and through dilational sites in competent granophyre

trap.evidence: Gold deposition by desulfidation and oxidation in stockwork vein systems

reservoir.evidence: Quartz vein stockworks within Golden Mile Dolerite granophyre

seal.evidence: No specific seal mentioned; alteration zones may provide some sealing

timing.evidence: 40Ar-39Ar age of 2602 ± 8 Ma from hydrothermal muscovite

preservation.evidence: Deposit preserved; no post-mineralization alteration evident

metallogenic_belt: Bushveld Complex, Kaapvaal Craton
tectonic_setting: Layered mafic-ultramafic intrusion within a continental craton

source.evidence: Primitive and evolved magmas from mantle-derived melts; PGE sourced from replenishing magma

transport.evidence: Lateral displacement of magma from feeder zone at Union section to distal facies

trap.evidence: Magma mixing triggers sulfur saturation and chromitite precipitation; sulfide segregation traps PGE

reservoir.evidence: Cumulate layers (chromitite, pyroxenite) host PGE; lateral thickness variation

seal.evidence: Overlying Main and Upper zone cumulates may act as seal; no detailed discussion

timing.evidence: Relative timing within Bushveld magmatic sequence; no absolute ages given

preservation.evidence: Intrusion preserved; minor hydrothermal overprint suggested by Os isotope variation

metallogenic_belt: Arabo-Nubian Shield, South Eastern Desert, Egypt
tectonic_setting: Neoproterozoic ophiolitic thrust complex, amphibolite facies metamorphism, synmetamorphic shear zone

source.evidence: PGE and base metals derived from original magmatic sulfide liquid; hydrothermal solutions from enclosing metasediments during metamorphism

transport.evidence: Transport by hydrothermal solutions along synmetamorphic shear zone; chloride complexes suggested for Cu and PGE

trap.evidence: Precipitation at sulfide-silicate contacts, fractures in plagioclase and garnet, and within massive sulfides due to decreasing temperature and fluid-rock interaction

reservoir.evidence: Hosted in mafic-ultramafic lens, with massive sulfides and stringers along shear plane

seal.evidence: No specific seal mentioned; mineralization hosted in metamorphic rocks with later carbonate veins

timing.evidence: Post-peak amphibolite facies metamorphism (650°C, 4-5 kbars); PGM precipitation during cooling from >500°C to ~250°C

preservation.evidence: Oxidized top part exploited in ancient times; deposit considered uneconomic; some supergene alteration

metallogenic_belt: Witwatersrand Basin, Kaapvaal Craton
tectonic_setting: Archean foreland basin on the Kaapvaal Craton, affected by regional metamorphism (greenschist to lower amphibolite facies)

source.evidence: Gold and sulfur sourced from detrital pyrite and possibly hydrothermal fluids; pyrrhotite formed by desulfidation of pyrite during metamorphism

transport.evidence: Fluid flow along permeable conglomerate horizons and fractures; metamorphic fluids involved

trap.evidence: Chemical trapping by reduction of fluids in contact with carbonaceous matter and pyrite; structural traps in conglomerate reefs

reservoir.evidence: Conglomerate reefs and quartzite provide porous and permeable host rocks

seal.evidence: Overlying Ventersdorp metabasalts and shale units act as seals

timing.evidence: Pyrrhotite formed during peak regional metamorphism at ca. 2716 Ma (age of Ventersdorp lavas); gold mineralization likely syn-metamorphic

preservation.evidence: Witwatersrand gold fields preserved in a stable cratonic setting; limited post-metamorphic deformation

metallogenic_belt: Quadrilátero Ferrífero, São Francisco Craton
tectonic_setting: Proterozoic cratonic basin, southern São Francisco Craton

source.evidence: Au and Pd possibly from underlying Rio das Velhas sequence or syngenetic in Itabira Group

transport.evidence: Hydrothermal fluids transporting Au and Pd as chloro-complexes

trap.evidence: Reaction with magnetite or Fe-dolomite causing precipitation; Mn oxides also involved

reservoir.evidence: Banded iron-formation (itabirite) and high-grade hematite ore as host

seal.evidence: No specific seal discussed; preservation likely due to structural setting

timing.evidence: No absolute ages given; relative timing post-metamorphism

preservation.evidence: Deposits preserved in Proterozoic rocks, but later deformation and oxidation noted

metallogenic_belt: Quadrilátero Ferrífero, southern São Francisco Craton
tectonic_setting: continental-shelf environment, Proterozoic Lake Superior-type iron-formation, lower amphibolite facies metamorphism

source.evidence: Au and Pd sourced from Archean Rio das Velhas Supergroup (basic to ultrabasic rocks and massive sulfide ore) based on Pb-Pb isotopes and geochemical anomalies

transport.evidence: Hydrothermal fluids transported Au and Pd as chloride complexes; D1 shearing provided pathways

trap.evidence: pH increase due to reaction between hydrothermal fluids and dolomitic itabirite (forming talc/phlogopite) caused Au and Pd precipitation; also possible redox reaction with magnetite

reservoir.evidence: Jacutinga layers (hydrothermally altered ironstone) and hematite veins host the ore

seal.evidence: No explicit seal discussed; preservation may be due to structural setting

timing.evidence: Mineralization synchronous with D1 shearing and prograde regional metamorphism, but no absolute ages given

preservation.evidence: Deposit preserved at Cauê mine; later weathering produced Mn oxides but not affecting primary ore

metallogenic_belt: Tuha Basin, southwestern margin, Xinjiang, NW China
tectonic_setting: Continental basin, internally drained, with basement of Proterozoic metasedimentary rocks and Paleozoic volcanic and granitic rocks

source.evidence: Uranium sourced from intermediate-acidic volcanic rocks and sandstones, leached by oxidizing groundwaters

transport.evidence: Uranium transported by migrating groundwaters through permeable sandstone aquifers, flow from southwest to northeast

trap.evidence: Reduction of uranium at oxidation-reduction boundaries by organic matter, H2S, and sulfides, forming tabular orebodies

reservoir.evidence: Hosted in porous Jurassic sandstone of Shuixigou Group, between impermeable mudstone strata

seal.evidence: Mudstone and shale aquitards overlie and underlie the aquifer, but no specific seal described

timing.evidence: U-Pb ages less than 1 Ma indicate ongoing mineralization, but no precise age given

preservation.evidence: Deposit preserved in arid climate, but no detailed discussion of post-mineralization changes

metallogenic_belt: Parnassos-Ghiona zone, Mediterranean karst bauxite belt
tectonic_setting: Carbonate platform within the Parnassos-Ghiona geotectonic zone

source.evidence: Al and Fe derived from weathering of underlying rocks; bacteria facilitate metal mobilization

transport.evidence: Percolating pore waters along faults; bacteria enhance mobility of Fe and other elements

trap.evidence: Carbonate rocks act as chemical trap; pH-Eh changes cause precipitation of Al and Fe minerals

reservoir.evidence: Bauxite horizons (B1, B2, B3) within limestone sequence; porous and permeable layers

seal.evidence: Overlying limestone and flysch provide cover; faults may breach seal locally

timing.evidence: Formed during Upper Jurassic to Middle Cretaceous; no absolute ages given

preservation.evidence: Deposits preserved despite tectonism; pyrite oxidation indicates some alteration

metallogenic_belt: Yangtze Craton, South China Block
tectonic_setting: Intracontinental extension related to subduction and rollback of the Paleo-Pacific Plate

metallogenic_belt: Mineiro Belt, southern São Francisco Craton
tectonic_setting: Late collisional orogenic stage, continental collision between Paleoproterozoic Mineiro Belt and Archean São Francisco Craton

metallogenic_belt: central Lhasa subterrane, Lhasa terrane, Tibet
tectonic_setting: continental arc formed by southward subduction of the Shiquanhe-Jiali Ocean

metallogenic_belt: Brasília Orogen, southernmost portion
tectonic_setting: Collisional orogen related to West Gondwana amalgamation, continental underthrusting

metallogenic_belt: North Qinling orogenic belt, Qinling Orogen
tectonic_setting: Subduction to post-collisional setting of the Shangdan Ocean and Erlangping back-arc basin

metallogenic_belt: Atlantis Bank, Southwest Indian Ridge
tectonic_setting: slow to ultra-slow spreading mid-ocean ridge, oceanic core complex

source.evidence: Gabbroic rocks, especially oxide gabbros, are enriched in S, Cu, Zn, Co; magmatic sulfides provide metal source.

transport.evidence: Seawater-derived hydrothermal fluids circulate along detachment faults and high-angle fractures; ductile and brittle deformation enhance permeability.

trap.evidence: Sulfide precipitation occurs due to cooling and fluid mixing; seafloor massive sulfides form at hydrothermal vents.

reservoir.evidence: Fractured and porous zones along detachment fault and in hanging wall provide space for sulfide accumulation.

seal.evidence: Low-temperature alteration minerals (smectite, carbonates) may seal fractures; no thick cap rock described.

timing.evidence: Metal mobilization occurs during amphibolite-greenschist facies metasomatism over ~2 Ma exhumation; SMS deposits form over tens of thousands of years.

preservation.evidence: Late-stage low-temperature alteration precipitates secondary sulfides; some sulfides may be reworked or oxidized.

metallogenic_belt: Karimnagar granulite belt, Eastern Dharwar Craton
tectonic_setting: Late Neoarchean convergent margin with E-W shortening, subduction-related arc setting

metallogenic_belt: Zavkhan Terrane, Central Asian Orogenic Belt
tectonic_setting: subduction zone, high-pressure metamorphism in a mélange zone

metallogenic_belt: Ivrea-Verbano Zone, Southern Alps, Adriatic plate
tectonic_setting: Continental rifting, post-collisional extension, precursor to Alpine Tethys opening
Triassic continental rift basin related to Pangea breakup, overlying Variscan basement of the South Portuguese Zone

metallogenic_belt: Rhenohercynian Zone, Variscan Orogen
tectonic_setting: restricted shallow marine back-arc basin in the Rheic Ocean, associated with mafic volcanism

source.evidence: Fe mobilised from footwall volcaniclastic rocks by H2CO3-rich fluids; mass-balance calculations show sufficient Fe in 136 m sequence

transport.evidence: Ascending fluids vented into marine basins or metasomatically replaced wall rocks; evidence of fluid flow through faults and permeable volcaniclastic rocks

trap.evidence: Chemical precipitation on seafloor (sedimentary-type) and metasomatic replacement of limestone (replacement-type); Fe-Si-rich gel formation and maturation

reservoir.evidence: Ore bodies up to 14 m thick, lateral extent up to 1 km; hosted in volcaniclastic rocks and limestone

seal.evidence: Hanging wall clastic sedimentary rocks and dolostone; low-grade metamorphic overprint preserved primary textures

timing.evidence: Middle to Late Devonian boundary (Givetian/Frasnian); associated with volcanic activity

preservation.evidence: Low-grade metamorphism (prehnite-pumpellyite to lower greenschist facies) preserved primary textures; Variscan deformation caused folding and faulting but did not destroy ore bodies

metallogenic_belt: Fore-Sudetic Monocline, Zechstein Basin
tectonic_setting: Fore-Sudetic Monocline, Zechstein (Werra) cycle, Permian basin

source.evidence: U and metals sourced from Rotliegend sandstone or Zechstein salt, remobilized by oxidizing fluids

transport.evidence: Fluid migration along redox boundary (Rote Fäule) and through permeable sandstone/shale

trap.evidence: Reduction of uranyl ions by organic matter (charcoal) and precipitation at oxic-anoxic boundary

reservoir.evidence: T1 dolomitic shale with high TOC, porous charred particles as adsorption sites

seal.evidence: Overlying Zechstein carbonates and evaporites may provide seal, but not explicitly discussed

timing.evidence: No reliable ages; previous U-Pb dates (Jurassic) considered unreliable due to Pb loss

preservation.evidence: Secondary oxidation (Rote Fäule) partially leached Cu sulfides; uraninite altered to secondary U minerals

metallogenic_belt: Eastern Tethyan metallogenic domain (Sanjiang belt, Gangdese belt, Tibetan Plateau)
tectonic_setting: Post-collisional setting after Neo-Tethys closure, involving lithospheric delamination and asthenospheric upwelling

source.evidence: Metasomatized lithospheric mantle-derived magmas enriched in chalcophile elements; primitive lamprophyres have Cu ~20-90 ppm, Pd ~0.5-3 ppb, Pt ~0.4-3 ppb

transport.evidence: High volatile contents (H2O ~4-5 wt%, Cl ~0.22 wt%) in ore-associated magmas; hydrothermal fluids released from magma transport metals

trap.evidence: Sulfide saturation and precipitation in upper crustal magma chambers; fluid exsolution and metal precipitation in porphyry systems

reservoir.evidence: Porphyry intrusions and surrounding host rocks; alteration haloes with secondary minerals

seal.evidence: No specific seal discussed; preservation likely due to post-collisional tectonic stability

timing.evidence: Eocene to Miocene ages (41-15 Ma) for ore-associated suites; ages from zircon U-Pb and whole-rock dating

preservation.evidence: Deposits exposed at surface; some alteration and mineralization preserved; no evidence of significant destruction

metallogenic_belt: Yeongnam massif, Daebo Orogeny
tectonic_setting: Jurassic Daebo Orogeny related to subduction of Paleo-Pacific (Izanagi) plate

source.evidence: Highly fractionated S-type granitic melt enriched in Li, F, B, P; crustal assimilation

transport.evidence: Faults and fractures; dike emplacement along N30°-70°E structures; immiscible melt-fluid phases

trap.evidence: Chemical traps: limestone host; physical traps: layered pegmatite zones; brecciation

reservoir.evidence: Layered pegmatite with border, intermediate, core zones; albitized zones

seal.evidence: Limestone and schist host rocks; brecciation indicates pressure release

timing.evidence: K-Ar dating of lepidolite: 169±3 to 155±3 Ma (Jurassic)

preservation.evidence: Exposed by Late Cretaceous uplift and erosion; some brecciation and albitization

metallogenic_belt: Delamerian Orogen, Tasmanides
tectonic_setting: Post-subduction setting related to Delamerian Orogeny, Andean-style convergent margin

source.evidence: Hydrous, oxidized, S- and Cl-rich magmas derived from post-subduction processes; zircon and apatite chemistry indicate fertile magmas

transport.evidence: Magmatic-hydrothermal fluids exsolved from evolved melts; amphibole-dominated fractionation suggests high water content

trap.evidence: Porphyry stocks and breccias; phyllic and potassic alteration zones; stockwork veins

reservoir.evidence: Granodiorite, diorite porphyry, and dacite porphyry host mineralization; shallow crustal emplacement

seal.evidence: No specific seal mentioned; overprinting alteration suggests some preservation

timing.evidence: Zircon U-Pb ages 490-465 Ma; molybdenite Re-Os ages ca. 480 Ma and 470-460 Ma

preservation.evidence: Prospects are greenfield; limited exploration; no economic deposits discovered yet

metallogenic_belt: Nanling Mountain Range, South China Block
tectonic_setting: Intracontinental setting related to Paleo-Pacific plate subduction, within the Cathaysia Block

source.evidence: REE sourced from allanite and other accessory minerals in granitic bedrock; LREE from magmatic allanite, M-HREE from Cl-rich deuteric fluids

transport.evidence: Cl-rich deuteric fluids transport M-HREE as chloride complexes; F- and carbonate-rich supergene fluids transport LREE

trap.evidence: Adsorption on kaolinite and halloysite in regolith; synchysite-(Ce) precipitation in weathered bedrock

reservoir.evidence: Regolith-hosted orebodies in completely weathered layer, thickness 2-14 m, controlled by geomorphology

seal.evidence: Humus and eluvial layers partially eroded; preservation in weathered profile depends on groundwater table

timing.evidence: Magmatic allanite crystallized at ca. 159 Ma; deuteric alteration at same time; supergene alteration postdates

preservation.evidence: Regolith preserved in subtropical climate; some erosion of upper layers; HREE may be lost to groundwater

structural_controls: contact between granite and metasedimentary schist
metallogenic_belt: Rocky Mountain Front Range, Longs Peak batholith, Colorado
tectonic_setting: anorogenic (A-type) peraluminous granite emplaced in Paleoproterozoic metamorphic rocks

source.evidence: Nd isotopes indicate mafic/ultramafic lower crust or upper mantle source, distinct from host granite

transport.evidence: Fluoride-silicate melt immiscibility during ascent and decompression; intrusion along foliation

trap.evidence: Immiscible melt segregation and crystallization; slow cooling allowed aggregation

reservoir.evidence: Hosted in aplite dikes and along contacts with metasedimentary schists

seal.evidence: No specific seal described; preservation likely due to rapid quenching in north, but F loss in south

timing.evidence: Sm-Nd age of 1421.9 ± 24.4 Ma on monazite and allanite; consistent with host granite age

preservation.evidence: Outcrops preserved; small size suggests sub-economic, but not significantly eroded

metallogenic_belt: Southeast Asian tin belt, Baoshan district, Sanjiang Tethyan domain
tectonic_setting: Neo-Tethyan subduction and subsequent India-Asia continental collision

source.evidence: Tin sourced from Late Cretaceous granite (Shiganghe) and ~32 Ma magmatism along Chongshan shear zone (Tiechang); crustal Pb isotope signatures

transport.evidence: Fluid transport via faults and fractures; ore fluids with δD and δ18O indicating magmatic-hydrothermal origin

trap.evidence: Structural traps: NW-trending faults and fracture zones; chemical traps: wallrock alteration (silicic, chlorite, sericite, tourmaline) and carbonate sequences for distal skarn

reservoir.evidence: Host rocks: Ordovician Zhibenshan pluton, Cambrian Hetaoping Formation, Neoproterozoic–Cambrian Chongshan Group; orebodies in fracture zones and altered dykes

seal.evidence: Impermeable wallrock alterations (silicic, carbonate) may act as seals; no explicit seal described

timing.evidence: Cassiterite U-Pb ages: 75.5±3.9 Ma and 75.9±4.8 Ma (Shiganghe); 32.8±1.3 Ma and 32.2±1.0 Ma (Tiechang); monazite U-Pb age: 32.2±1.0 Ma

preservation.evidence: Deposits preserved at current erosion level; no evidence of significant post-mineralization disruption

metallogenic_belt: Yanbian area, Central Asian Orogenic Belt
tectonic_setting: Extensional setting related to subduction of Paleo-Pacific Ocean plate

source.evidence: Magmatic sulfur isotopic composition (δ34S 1.6-3.9‰) and fluid δ18O (2.4-5.5‰) indicate predominantly magmatic source.

transport.evidence: Hydrothermal fluids transported metals via fractures and veins; water-rock interaction facilitated metal transport.

trap.evidence: Chlorite-sericite alteration zone hosts mineralization; water-rock interaction and cooling triggered metal precipitation.

reservoir.evidence: Late Permian diorite and Early Cretaceous granitic complex provide host rock; stockwork and veinlet-hosted mineralization.

seal.evidence: No specific seal mentioned; post-mineralization diorite porphyry dike may provide some cover.

timing.evidence: Biotite 40Ar/39Ar plateau age 112.82±0.28 Ma and titanite SIMS U-Pb intercept age 109.0±2.4 Ma constrain mineralization at ~110 Ma.

preservation.evidence: Deposit preserved; cut by post-mineralization dike but ore bodies remain intact.

metallogenic_belt: Uluguru Mountains, East African Orogen
tectonic_setting: Intracontinental rift-related carbonatite within Neoproterozoic orogenic belt, associated with East African Rift System

source.evidence: Mantle source indicated by C-O and Mg isotopes; δ13C -4.1 to -6.2‰, δ18O +6.5 to +7.31‰, δ26Mg -0.44 to +0.19‰; no recycled carbon

transport.evidence: Magmatic fractionation and carbothermal fluids; dike emplacement along fractures; fenitization indicates fluid-rock interaction

trap.evidence: Burbankite crystallization in late magmatic-transition stage; pseudomorphs after burbankite; REE-fluorocarbonates, barite, strontianite precipitation

reservoir.evidence: Dolomite carbonatite breccia and apatite dolomite carbonatite as host; REE-bearing dikes crosscut earlier units

seal.evidence: No explicit seal described; fenite aureole may provide some containment

timing.evidence: No absolute ages reported; relative timing from field relationships

preservation.evidence: Surface processes caused bastnaesite enrichment; pseudomorphs well-preserved; some supergene alteration

metallogenic_belt: East Kunlun Orogenic Belt, Central East Kunlun belt
tectonic_setting: Accretionary orogenic belt related to Proto- and Paleo-Tethys oceanic subduction and continental collision

source.evidence: Multiple external crustal sulfur sources indicated by δ34S values (0.5‰ to 7.5‰) and radiogenic Os isotopes (γOs up to 3531)

transport.evidence: Sulfide droplets transported by flowing magma; orthopyroxene oikocrysts and olivine entrained and redeposited

trap.evidence: Coalescence of sulfide droplets in interstitial framework of orthopyroxene cumulate; capillary effects trap blebs in pore spaces

reservoir.evidence: Coarse-grained orthopyroxenite and lherzolite host sulfides; pore bodies up to 3000 μm radius facilitate large bleb formation

seal.evidence: No explicit seal discussed; preservation inferred from deposit existence

timing.evidence: No direct age data; intrusion age ~420 Ma from regional context

preservation.evidence: Deposit preserved; later alteration (serpentinization) but sulfides intact

metallogenic_belt: Rhenohercynian Zone, Variscan orogenic belt, Lahn Syncline, Rhenish Massif
tectonic_setting: rift-related crustal extension, intraplate volcanic activity in a restricted marine basin

source.evidence: Fe sourced from alkali basaltic volcaniclastic rocks via low-temperature fluid-rock interaction; mass balance indicates Fe mobilisation

transport.evidence: Diffuse low-temperature venting of reduced, near-neutral to slightly alkaline fluids; fluid mixing with seawater

trap.evidence: Rapid Fe(II) oxidation and Fe-(oxyhydr)oxide precipitation in seawater column; formation of Fe-Si-rich gel on seafloor

reservoir.evidence: Ore bodies form lenses up to 5 m thick on top of volcaniclastic sequences; haematite-quartz and siderite-haematite ores

seal.evidence: Hanging wall clastic sedimentary rocks, tuff, limestone, breccias; but no specific seal described

timing.evidence: Formation near Middle to Late Devonian boundary (~383 Ma); during waning volcanism

preservation.evidence: Low metamorphic overprint (<300°C, prehnite-pumpellyite to lower greenschist facies); Variscan deformation caused folding and thrusting

metallogenic_belt: Fore-Sudetic Monocline, Kupferschiefer district, Poland
tectonic_setting: Zechstein basin, oxidized zone boundary

source.evidence: U-Th-rich hydrothermal fluids, copper-bearing solutions

transport.evidence: Hydrothermal fluids along permeable sandstone and shale horizons

trap.evidence: Oxic-anoxic boundary within T1 dolomitic shale, geochemical control

reservoir.evidence: Sandstone and shale host rocks, pipe-like structures

seal.evidence: Overlying Zechstein evaporites may provide seal

timing.evidence: Post-sedimentary, related to hydrothermal events, no precise ages given

preservation.evidence: Thucholite preserved, but oxidation alteration present

metallogenic_belt: Lubin district, Fore-Sudetic Monocline
tectonic_setting: Zechstein basin, post-Variscan foreland

source.evidence: Oxidised metal-bearing solutions from Upper Rotliegend sandstone

transport.evidence: Migration along faults and permeable units

trap.evidence: Thucholite concretions in T1 shale, with Cu sulphide inclusions

reservoir.evidence: Shale matrix and pores

seal.evidence: Evaporite units (Lower Anhydrite) may act as seal

timing.evidence: No absolute ages provided

preservation.evidence: Thucholite preserved, but secondary enrichment indicated

tectonic_setting: Continental arc on the Okhotsk Block, later separated and translated; subduction of Izanagi and Pacific Plates
Active orogenic wedge in Arabia-Eurasia collision zone, with subduction beneath eastern Greater Caucasus

metallogenic_belt: Domeyko Cordillera, Central Andes, northern Chile
tectonic_setting: Positive tectonic inversion of Mesozoic rift basins in a subduction-related orogen (Central Andes forearc)
Passive continental margin, post-rift, affected by hotspot passage
N-S extension in continental Greece, active rift with horst-and-graben topography

metallogenic_belt: Eastern Betics Shear Zone (EBSZ), SE Iberia
tectonic_setting: Transpressive tectonic corridor accommodating Africa-Eurasia convergence; reverse-strike slip fault zone in SE Iberia
Intracontinental rift system within the North China Craton, related to India-Asia collision and Pacific Plate subduction
Active mountain front of the eastern Southern Alps, a retro-belt of the Alps formed since the Middle Miocene, with ongoing Adria-Europe convergence.
Western Tibetan Plateau, at intersection of Tianshuihai, Qiangtang, and Lhasa terranes, bounded by Karakoram fault and Bangong-Nujiang suture

metallogenic_belt: Attic-Cycladic Crystalline Massif, Hellenic orogen
tectonic_setting: Extensional exhumation of HP-LT rocks in a back-arc setting due to slab rollback

metallogenic_belt: Wuyi-Yunkai orogen, South China Block
tectonic_setting: Intracontinental orogeny within the South China Block, Early Paleozoic Wuyi-Yunkai orogen
